preamble: The voiced retroflex flap [ɽ] shifts to the voiced retroflex lateral approximant [ɭ] through manner change and lateralization in South Asian languages. This transformation represents a rare phonetic pathway between retroflex consonant types, documented in allophonic variation and rapid speech contexts in Dravidian and Indo-Aryan languages.

examples.note: Fast speech variant in retroflex-conditioned environments
Intervocalic allophone in certain morphological contexts
